Title: [PRD-UX-001] "What's next" post-connection experience
Description: After adding a context source, user is guided to at least 1 actionable next step (e.g., "Try asking your AI tool about [component]"). No dead-end experience. Discussions ongoing — currently in design phase.

Title: [PRD-UX-002] End-to-end admin workflow
Description: Admin can complete full flow: add source → configure → see ingestion status → view conflicts → resolve conflicts → verify MCP output. No step requires CLI or direct DB access. Spans ENG-007/009.

Title: [PRD-DEM-001] Demo assets
Description: At minimum: scripted video walkthrough or live demo environment showing IPE flow (add source → context ingested → conflict surfaced → use in AI tool → better output). Usable by AE/SE without product team present.

Title: [PRD-DEM-002] Competitive battle cards
Description: 1-page positioning against: IBM Carbon MCP, NY State MCP, and "build your own MCP" approaches. Includes differentiation points, talk track, and objection responses.

Title: [PRD-QA-001] Enterprise-scale testing
Description: IPE tested with enterprise-scale data: realistic source volume (not just internal test data), concurrent users, and data complexity. At least 1 test with non-Knapsack-internal workspace.

Title: [PRD-QA-002] CX product knowledge transfer
Description: CX team has completed hands-on training with IPE. Can: demo the product, troubleshoot basic issues, answer top 10 customer questions, and support onboarding without escalating to product.

Title: [PRD-QA-003] Go/No-Go decision gate
Description: All Blocker-priority requirements in this PRD are green. Executive sign-off documented. Fallback plan defined if any Blocker is not met.

Title: [PRD-QA-004] MCP beta feedback collection
Description: Structured process for collecting and triaging MCP beta feedback from internal and early-access users. Includes: feedback intake form, severity classification, weekly triage, and clear criteria for "beta-blocking" vs. "post-GA." Must inform QA-005 graduation criteria.

Title: [PRD-QA-005] MCP graduation plan (beta → GA)
Description: Documented plan for transitioning MCP from beta to GA. Includes: graduation criteria (stability, performance, coverage), feature flag removal strategy, customer communication plan, and rollback procedure.

Title: [PRD-SAL-001] Pricing & positioning training
Description: 1-hour session delivered to all AE/SE/CX. Covers: Platform + Consumption model, "included not free" positioning, adoption-first selling, overage expansion model. Recorded for async access.

Title: [PRD-SAL-002] IPE quick reference card
Description: Single page with: value prop, 3 personas, 4 use cases, pricing tier summary, and key objection responses. Printable for sales calls.

Title: [PRD-SAL-003] Objection handling guide
Description: Top 10 objections with scripted responses. Must include: "Why not build our own MCP?", "What's the ROI?", "Why consumption pricing?", "Token cost concerns", and security/compliance objections.

Title: [PRD-SAL-004] Discovery call script
Description: Structured discovery flow: opener, PCRI validation questions, AI readiness signals, deployment tier signals, next steps. Tested by at least 1 AE before GA.

Title: [PRD-SAL-005] Email templates (3 sequences)
Description: Published and approved: 1) Existing customer IPE intro, 2) New prospect cold outreach, 3) Post-demo follow-up. All use "IPE included" messaging.

Title: [PRD-SAL-006] CX adoption playbook
Description: 30-60-90 day adoption milestones defined. Usage triggers for expansion conversation documented. QBR template focused on consumption growth.

Title: [PRD-SAL-007] Customer overview deck
Description: 10-slide presentation covering: what is IPE, why now, how it works, pricing overview, getting started. Usable for first call or email attachment.

Title: [PRD-DOC-001] Remove "Freemium" language
Description: All documents updated: "Free" → "Tier 0" or "Included." "Freemium" → "Platform + Consumption." Applies to: Offering Kit, Onboarding Kit, Index page, ROI Calculator.

Title: [PRD-DOC-002] Align SLA tiers to consumption tiers
Description: SLA table in Offering Kit maps Essentials/Pro/Enterprise to Tier 0/1/2/3. No orphaned tier references.

Title: [PRD-ANL-004] Adoption metric definition
Description: Formal definition of what constitutes "adoption" for IPE. Must answer: What counts as an active user? What is a "meaningful" operation vs. a health check? What usage threshold = "adopted"? This is upstream of ANL-003 (dashboard) and all commercial reporting. Usage data exists back to September 2025.

Title: [DECISION] Consumption tier boundaries and price points
Description: Tier 1/2/3 thresholds and dollar amounts. Current ranges ($24-48K) too wide to quote. Requires operation costing data from Grant first. Blocks: all GTM materials, AE enablement, CRM configuration.

Title: [DECISION] Tier 0 usage quota number
Description: Specific operation count included in platform fee. Must be "enough to see value, not unlimited." Requires understanding of current MCP usage patterns (highest site: 195 requests/2 weeks).

Title: [DECISION] ToS amendment vs. re-acceptance approach
Description: Do existing customers re-accept terms entirely, or can we use addendums? Impacts engineering work on acceptance tracking mechanism.

Title: [DECISION] Enterprise test account for QA
Description: Need non-internal data with enterprise-scale source volume, concurrent users, and data complexity for pre-GA testing.

Title: [DECISION] MDM strategy scope for V1
Description: Grant flagged need for master data management strategy across PPP. What's required for V1 vs. what can wait?

Title: [DECISION] Feature flag approach for MCP GA
Description: MCP is currently behind a feature flag. Does V1 GA mean feature flag removal? Or keep for controlled rollout? What are the criteria for removing the flag?

Title: [DECISION] Storybook access gating during beta
Description: Storybook ingestion is a V1 source type, but should it be gated during MCP beta? If gated, need criteria for opening access. Impacts ENG-002 acceptance criteria and QA-004 beta feedback scope.

Title: [DECISION] SLA tier mapping to consumption tiers
Description: Do SLA levels (response times, uptime guarantees) map directly to consumption tiers, or are they independent? Current Offering Kit shows Essentials/Pro/Enterprise SLA levels that don't clearly align with Tier 0/1/2/3 consumption model.
